design: DESIGN.md §6 — gate is the ratio vs legacy on identical inputs; integrated engine pays adapter cost inside (here beyond) the raw-pipeline headroom
supersedes: spike/benchmark split-JVM OLD/NEW harness (predated integration, measured the raw unadapted pipeline)
harness: maven-resolution-plan/phase0-tools IntegratedBenchmark: MavenParser end-to-end, one JVM, ctx engine=legacy vs engine=maven over an identical reactor input set; warm iterations zero-download (RecordingHttpSender record-once/replay).

environment.note: camel rows run at -Xmx768m (see fdLeak); maven-15 at -Xmx2g. Ratio within each tier is same-heap for both engines and therefore fair; maven camel absolute numbers are a GC-pressured upper bound.

protocol.warm: fresh MavenPomCache per iteration, zero network (replay); median of iters
protocol.loop: warm-cache re-resolution: one shared warm MavenPomCache, re-resolve the full reactor repeatedly (UpdateMavenModel steady state); median per iteration
protocol.loopNote: deviates from the baseline's per-leaf loop — a standalone leaf cannot resolve its reactor parent from the remote store; re-resolving the full reactor under a warm shared cache is the closest self-consistent analogue. Both engines use the identical shared-cache protocol, so the ratio holds.

fdLeak.finding: maven mode accumulates OS file descriptors within a single large-reactor parse (GC-reclaimable FileInputStream handles from materialized-pom / model-source reads outpace GC), exhausting the 92160 per-process cap at ~47 BOM-heavy camel modules.
fdLeak.evidence: camel-40/47/50 crash with 'Too many open files' at -Xmx2g (both --daemon and --no-daemon, shell ulimit 1048576); the same tiers complete at -Xmx768m because frequent GC reclaims the FileInputStream FDs before the cap. maven-15 (shallow deps) stays under the cap at 2g.
fdLeak.classification: flip-blocker — an FD-lifecycle fix in the descriptor/model file-handle path is required before maven mode can resolve a BOM-heavy reactor at production heap sizes.
fdLeak.notFixed: measurement slice; no speculative engine change made (DESIGN discipline).
